rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Ensure AGREE annex blocks are properly scoped within their respective component types or implementations
Preserve the original AADL architecture by not altering the component hierarchy or connections
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place assumptions or guarantees in implementation annexes
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
